<!-- GENERATED SUPPORT FILE. Do not edit here. Run `node scripts/sync-skill-support.mjs` from the agent-skills repo root. -->

# Eval Loop Spec

> Loop-centered artifact system for measurable strategy -> execution -> evaluation work.

## Purpose

The original artifact system organizes by producer skill. That works for one-shot artifacts, but it is weak for measurable initiatives because the real unit of improvement is not a skill; it is the loop: a landing page, ad set, email sequence, social series, campaign, or other surface with an observable metric.

Eval loops make the improvement unit explicit. They borrow the useful operating model from `autoresearch`: fixed scope, fixed evaluator, one mutable surface, a results ledger, and keep/discard decisions. They do not borrow the unsafe parts for marketing/content: endless unattended loops, over-trusting a single noisy metric, or rewriting execution surfaces without approval.

## One Scaffold, Many Evaluators

`eval-loop` is the single scaffold and ledger entrypoint. It creates/resumes the loop workspace, defines the measurable surface, records the metric contract, and owns `program.md`, `context.md`, `results.tsv`, and `learnings.md`.

It is not the universal evaluator. Surface-specific eval skills produce the actual measurement artifacts under `evals/`:

| Surface | Evaluator |
|---|---|
| Short-form video / social post | `short-form-eval` |
| Landing page conversion | `lp-eval` |
| Paid ads | future `ad-eval` |
| Email / cold outreach | future `email-eval` or `outreach-eval` |
| Multi-channel campaign | future `campaign-eval` |

This avoids a bloated mega-evaluator while preserving one obvious entrypoint for loop setup and learning promotion.

## Directory Contract

Every measurable initiative lives under `skills-resources/loops/`. Loops are organized by measurable initiative, not by the skill or domain that produced the latest artifact:

```text
.agents/skill-artifacts/
└── loops/
    └── {loop-slug}/
        ├── program.md
        ├── context.md
        ├── strategy/
        ├── execution/
        ├── evals/
        ├── results.tsv
        └── learnings.md
```

Loop ownership rules:

- Name the loop after the measurable surface or initiative: `pricing-page`, `founder-outreach-q3`, `activation-onboarding`, `short-form-series`.
- Put strategy, execution, evals, results, and learnings for that initiative in the same loop folder even when different stacks produce them.
- Keep one-shot outputs outside loops under `.agents/skill-artifacts/{mkt,product,research,meta}/`.

### File Roles

| Path | Role | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| `program.md` | Operating instructions: goal, metric, mutable surface, guardrails, cycle protocol, promotion rule | `eval-loop` |
| `context.md` | Loop-local assumptions, baseline, canonical artifact links, constraints, open questions | `eval-loop` plus downstream skills |
| `strategy/` | Strategy artifacts that define what should change: briefs, hypotheses, plans, test designs | Strategy skills |
| `execution/` | Marketing/content assets ready to publish elsewhere: copy variants, posts, ad sets, sequences | Execution skills |
| `evals/` | Post-execution evidence: metric snapshots, qualitative review, source citations, confidence | Evaluation skills |
| `results.tsv` | One row per cycle; compact keep/discard/watch/blocked ledger | Evaluation skills |
| `learnings.md` | Promoted lessons that future cycles should reuse | Evaluation skills, critic-approved |

### Learning Promotion To Experience

`learnings.md` is loop-local by default. Promote a learning into `.agents/experience/` only after it clears the Quality Feedback Protocol: the source result is `keep`, the metric source and confidence are explicit, the lesson is reusable beyond one execution artifact, and its scope is named.

Promoted learnings should be appended to the relevant experience file (`audience.md`, `business.md`, `product.md`, `brand.md`, or `goals.md`) with the source loop/eval path, confidence, applicability boundary, and "do not apply when" caveat. Do not promote `watch`, `discard`, `blocked`, or confounded findings.

Use `references/_shared/quality-dashboard-spec.md` for aggregate quality tracking when a project has repeated evals, critic overrides, or rubric calibration findings. The loop folder remains the evidence source; the dashboard is only a compact trend index.

## Where Loops Sit

The stack uses three artifact surfaces. Loops are deliberately separate from one-shot skill outputs:

- `.agents/manifest.json` and `.agents/artifact-index.md` — infrastructure, regenerated by `manifest-sync`
- `.agents/experience/` — cross-skill Q&A substrate (append-only, not loop-scoped)
- `.agents/skill-artifacts/meta/` — cross-domain process artifacts (specs, decisions, sketches, records, tasks.md, roadmap.md)
- `.agents/skill-artifacts/{mkt,product,research}/` — one-shot pipeline outputs by domain (briefs, copy, plans, audits)
- `skills-resources/loops/[slug]/` — measurable strategy -> execution -> evaluation workspaces
- `.agents/skill-artifacts/.archive/` — cleanup-artifacts target (never deleted)

Top-level `brand/`, `research/`, and `architecture/` remain canonical sources of truth — human-maintained, not agent-generated, and they live outside `.agents/skill-artifacts/`.

`manifest-sync` walks `.agents/skill-artifacts/`, `skills-resources/loops/`, `research/`, `brand/`, and `architecture/`.

## Frontmatter

Every markdown artifact inside a loop follows the normal manifest contract:

```yaml
---
skill: lp-brief
version: 1
date: 2026-05-13
status: done
summary: "Pricing page v2 conversion hypothesis"
purpose: "Defines the page changes to test in cycle 2"
lifecycle: strategy
use_when: "Creating or evaluating cycle-2 pricing page execution"
do_not_use_when: "The pricing offer or target segment has changed"
upstream: "skills-resources/loops/pricing-page/context.md, brand/DESIGN.md, research/icp-research.md"
downstream: "skills-resources/loops/pricing-page/execution/, skills-resources/loops/pricing-page/evals/"
---
```

Recommended lifecycle values inside loops:

| Lifecycle | Path | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `loop` | `program.md` | Operating contract for the loop |
| `loop-context` | `context.md` | Local substrate for assumptions, baselines, constraints |
| `strategy` | `strategy/*.md` | Plan, brief, hypothesis, test design |
| `execution` | `execution/*.md` | Publish-ready marketing/content artifact |
| `evaluation` | `evals/*.md` | Measurement snapshot or post-launch review |
| `learning` | `learnings.md` | Promoted reusable evidence |

## Results Ledger

`results.tsv` is intentionally small and append-only:

```text
cycle	date	artifact	primary_metric	value	baseline	status	description
1	2026-05-13	evals/2026-05-13-cycle-1.md	conversion_rate	3.4%	2.9%	keep	Sharper hero offer improved pricing page trial starts
```

Prefer the helper over hand-editing:

```bash
bun scripts/append-loop-result.ts pricing-page \
  --artifact evals/2026-05-13-cycle-1.md \
  --metric conversion_rate \
  --value 3.4% \
  --baseline 2.9% \
  --status keep \
  --description "Sharper hero offer improved pricing page trial starts"
```

The helper validates the header, status enum, cycle uniqueness, artifact existence, safe relative paths, and tab/newline safety before appending.

Allowed status values:

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `keep` | Clear improvement, useful simplification, or strong evidence worth carrying forward |
| `discard` | Worse result, confounded result, or complexity without measurable upside |
| `watch` | Promising but underpowered; do not promote yet |
| `blocked` | Missing metric, attribution, measurement window, or execution proof |

## Skill Placement Rules

Use loop placement when all three are true:

1. The work has a measurable real-world surface.
2. The output may be revised over multiple cycles.
3. Future strategy or execution should read prior results before acting.

Keep using non-loop `.agents/skill-artifacts/` subfolders — `.agents/skill-artifacts/meta/`, `.agents/skill-artifacts/{marketing,product,research}/` (outside `loops/`) — when the output is a one-time audit, decision record, spec, task list, cleanup report, or non-measurable pipeline artifact.

Keep using top-level canonical folders when the artifact is the durable source of truth for a domain and is updated in place by humans.

## Execution Skills

Execution skills are in scope only where this stack can produce the actual asset better than an external build/design tool. Current allowed execution domains:

- Marketing copy and content
- Social posts and short-form packages
- Email and cold outreach sequences
- Ad copy and campaign asset matrices
- SEO content briefs or publish-ready page copy

Execution skills do not deploy pages, edit product code, design full interfaces, or publish to third-party platforms. They write publish-ready artifacts under `execution/` and leave deployment/publishing external.

## Evaluation Skills

Evaluation applies only to measurable outputs. Evaluate the surface, not the producing skill.

Examples:

- Landing page conversion -> `evals/[date]-cycle-N.md`
- Meta ads -> CTR, CPA, ROAS, policy/claim caveats
- Social/short-form -> retention, saves, shares, CTR, qualitative pattern deltas
- Cold outreach/email -> reply rate, positive reply rate, meeting booked rate, deliverability caveats
- Campaign -> traffic, leads, revenue, channel mix, spend efficiency

Research artifacts such as ICP or market research are not evaluated with generic taste rubrics. They are evaluated through downstream usefulness: cite frequency, repeated contradiction from loop evidence, operator reports of off-target outputs, or obvious staleness after a market shift. When that threshold is met, write the review under the relevant loop's `evals/` folder or `.agents/skill-artifacts/research/evals/` if the evidence spans multiple loops. Update canonical `research/` only as a separate, explicit revision after the evidence is accepted.

`eval-loop` may route to the relevant evaluator, but it should not fake surface-specific scoring itself. If no evaluator exists yet, it creates the loop and marks the next eval step as a gap rather than inventing a generic rubric.

## Scaffold Command

Create a loop workspace:

```bash
bun scripts/scaffold-eval-loop.ts "pricing page conversion"
```

This creates the loop folder, writes starter `program.md`, `context.md`, `learnings.md`, `results.tsv`, and runs `manifest-sync`.

Append a validated result row:

```bash
bun scripts/append-loop-result.ts "pricing-page" \
  --artifact evals/2026-05-13-cycle-1.md \
  --metric conversion_rate \
  --value 3.4% \
  --baseline 2.9% \
  --status keep \
  --description "Sharper hero offer improved pricing page trial starts"
```

Update the quality dashboard when protocol thresholds are met:

```bash
bun scripts/update-quality-dashboard.ts \
  --loop pricing-page \
  --latest-cycle 1 \
  --latest-status keep \
  --primary-metric conversion_rate \
  --latest-value 3.4% \
  --quality-risk low \
  --next-action "Carry sharper hero proof into cycle 2"
```

## Guardrails

- Do not run autonomous marketing/content loops indefinitely. Human approval is required before publishing or materially changing a live external surface.
- Do not promote a lesson from a single weak or confounded metric snapshot.
- Do not promote loop learnings to `.agents/experience/` without a `keep` result, confidence note, and named applicability boundary.
- Do not mutate canonical `brand/`, `research/`, or `architecture/` from a loop. Propose updates separately when evidence accumulates.
- Do not create one loop per skill. Create one loop per measurable initiative.
- Do not create a loop if there is no metric, no baseline path, and no future cycle.

## Completion Status

Loop artifacts use the same status values as every other artifact: `done`, `done_with_concerns`, `blocked`, `needs_context`.
